theme: E4 cycle 8 — a measure/iterate cycle (design-spec rows 155-158) closing the two still-undone named themes (responsive/mobile + copy tightening) plus the c156 orphan-shelf 'didn't' and a join-scent lift, PLUS an operator-added fifth thread: swap the homepage hero back toward the OLD aDNA banner (DNA helixes rising from computers) via III image planning + versions. Rubric was already GREEN after c156; this cycle iterates without a confirming-lens Workflow (operator decision — saved for c158/c159). III 7-step.
register: Tokyo Night dark-first; <=2 fighting colors (cyan --color-link / purple --color-primary) — the band-4 cue ticks + orphan-cluster border reuse the existing cyan/muted tokens, no new accent; no new motion; no marketing adjectives; honest topology (orphan set stays NAMED + now visually subordinated, zero fabricated edges); the new homepage hero keeps the SS-Ghibli pixel register (still pixel-art; no baked text — the title composites live).
operator_scope: Plan-gate AskUserQuestion: (1) NO confirming-lens Workflow this cycle (direct iterate; save the lens for c158/c159). (2) Scope = all four iterate threads (responsive/mobile + copy tightening + orphan-shelf polish + join-scent lift). (3) FIFTH operator-added thread: adjust the homepage hero back toward the old DNA-helix-from-computers banner via III planning + versions. (4) Mid-cycle pick gate: operator chose hero candidate r1_H2_v1 (helix -> network bridge). COMMIT-ONLY (E4 bundles its deploy later).

iii_7step.measure: git pull already-up-to-date (HEAD cfdec25 / c156). Baseline build 162pp/0err; gates green-by-inheritance (the clean pull changed nothing). Network-Legibility rubric GREEN after c156: node_clarity 4.5 / edge_honesty 5 / local_shared 5 / navigability 4.75 / join_scent 4 (lowest). The two design-spec measure/iterate themes still undone = responsive/mobile + copy tightening; c156 deferred the orphan-shelf layout 'didn't' + an optional confirming lens.
iii_7step.orient: Read the 3 E4 surfaces + the hero stack. /network had only @media 700px+480px; gate-9 covered /network but NOT /vaults/graph or /vaults/[slug] (the 40-node Mermaid is the highest mobile risk). The hero swap is E1(closed) brand territory — operator-directed exception; HomeHero's default heroAlt describes the OLD isometric map and /network passes its own alt, so a homepage swap only needs index.astro (import + explicit alt). gate-11 requires the hero alt to match /pixel-art|wordmark/ — the SS-Ghibli helix is still pixel-art, so an honest 'pixel-art' alt keeps it green.
iii_7step.select: Six threads: A1 responsive (add gate-9 coverage for /vaults/graph + /vaults/[slug], fix any overflow), A2 copy (tighten the /network hero lead), A3 orphan-shelf (subordinate the 'Not yet linked' cluster via a muted dashed border — light touch, NOT a custom-component rebuild; the layout + Perf fix pair for c159), A4 join-scent (front-load a commitment/safety cue row on band-4), B (clone the e1 hero runner -> e1_hero_helix_gen.py, generate helix candidates, operator picks, swap winner into the homepage).
iii_7step.implement: A1: gate-9-responsive.spec.ts pages[] += /vaults/graph + /vaults/Harness.aDNA (the island's overflow-x:auto + svg max-width:100% already contain the wide graph — validated, not redesigned). A2: network.astro hero lead 75->~50 words (kept the inline 'vault' definition from c155; dropped the trailing registry clause band-2 covers). A3: graph.astro :global(.mermaid-container .cluster rect) muted-token dashed stroke (parked-holding-area cue). A4: network.astro band-4 .run-meta cue row ('About five minutes' / 'Needs git + the Claude Code CLI' / 'Local-first - nothing leaves until you choose') with cyan tick ::before, + trimmed the now-duplicated step-1 run-note (kept the Get-started link). FIRST .run-meta attempt used a negative top margin that OVERLAPPED the multi-line subtitle (caught by eyeball, not gates) -> fixed to a positive margin + a tighter .net-run .section-subtitle. B: e1_hero_helix_gen.py (3 helix directions H1/H2/H3, GHIBLI_TAIL reused, Ultra+fast-fallback); smoke H1 OK ($0.04), r1 fan-out 4/6 OK ($0.16; 2 hit Imagen's known empty-return, their pair variants succeeded); operator picked r1_H2_v1 -> published as hero_adna_helix.png + index.astro import swap + explicit pixel-art heroAlt.
iii_7step.re_measure: build 162pp/0err. Full Playwright suite 86/86 (baseline 76 + 10 new gate-9 E4-surface tests; STATE's '73' was stale). axe gate-4 0 WCAG-AA BOTH modes on /network, /vaults/graph, /vaults/Harness.aDNA, homepage. Hero gates 10+11 green (incl. the alt-text gate now matching 'pixel-art'; aspect 1.778). Lighthouse desktop: home 100/96/100/100 (Perf 100 = hero-swap perf-neutral; A11y 96 = the pre-existing E1-baseline target-size item, NOT a regression — axe is 0 AA) / /network 100/100/100/100 (up from the 98 baseline) / /vaults/graph 100/100/100/100 (the c156 ~89 was the throttled-method dagre cost; Mermaid renders post-paint so the desktop preset reads clean — the c159 prerender deferral still stands as the robustness fix). Eyeballed dark+light, desktop+mobile (390px): band-4 cue row reads clean (post-fix), orphan cluster reads as a parked dashed holding area, homepage helix->network hero reads with the title legible over the scrim.
iii_7step.record: this JSON + session cycle log + STATE/mission cascade

self_score.method: Rosetta self-re-score (operator decision: no lens Workflow this cycle). Owning-lens framing retained from c155/c156.

rationale.node_clarity_4.5_to_4.75: The /network hero lead is tightened (75->~50 words) while keeping the inline 'vault' definition — the 'an aDNA computer = one machine + many vaults under one Home.aDNA' thought lands faster. Not a full 5 (self-score conservatism; the band-2 definitional copy is unchanged).
rationale.join_scent_4_to_4.5: Band-4 now front-loads the commitment + safety as a scannable cue row (time, prereqs, local-first) BEFORE the steps, so 'what does it cost me to participate / is it safe' is answerable at a glance — the c155 Node-Operator concern's next increment. Not 5 because the join scent still lives only on /network (no homepage participation path; a c158/E-later item).
rationale.edge_honesty_held_5: The orphan 'Not yet linked' cluster now reads as a deliberately PARKED holding area (muted dashed border) — reinforces the c156 structural win; zero fabricated edges. Held at 5.
rationale.navigability_held_4.75: Responsive coverage added (gate-9 now guards /vaults/graph + /vaults/[slug] at 320/375px; the surfaces are contained at mobile), but the graph is small-but-whole on mobile (the keyboard twin + index remain the nav path) — held at 4.75; a confirming IA/Newcomer lens could move it to 5 (c158).
rationale.local_shared_held_5: No change to the band-2 boundary content; held at 5.

self_score.result: No rubric item < 4; two items nudged up (node_clarity 4.5->4.75, join_scent 4->4.5). Rubric GREEN, mean lifted. A confirming lens Workflow (c158) can independently verify before the c159 close.

deploy: NOT deployed — commit-only (operator standing instruction; the whole E4 surface bundles its deploy later). The new homepage helix hero goes LIVE at the next deploy. #needs-human: rotate SS_VERCEL_TOKEN (recurring CLI-stdout leak class; carry-forward).

aar_lightweight.worked: Kicking off the image-gen fan-out in the background while doing the 4 code threads parallelized the cycle well (candidates were ready by the time the code was done). The smoke-test-first discipline validated the runner (auth + API + output) for $0.04 before the $0.16 fan-out. Eyeballing (not just gating) caught the band-4 negative-margin overlap that every gate passed through.
aar_lightweight.didnt: The .run-meta negative top margin overlapped the multi-line centered subtitle — a reminder that negative margins can't see a flexible neighbor's height. And 2/6 Imagen calls hit the silent 'no images returned' empty-return (the known E1 quirk) — the pair variants saved the round, but it's an argument for always fanning out >=2 variants per direction.
aar_lightweight.finding: Lighthouse Perf for the client-rendered Mermaid graph is measurement-method-dependent: the c156 throttled method read ~89, the desktop preset reads 100 (Mermaid renders post-paint). The honest read is 'fine on desktop, soft on heavy throttling' — which is exactly why the c159 prerender deferral is the right robustness call, not a Perf-gate panic.
aar_lightweight.change: When adding a stacked element under a centered multi-line block, set spacing on the block (reduce its margin-bottom) rather than pulling the new element up with a negative margin. Keep fanning image-gen to >=2 variants/direction to absorb Imagen empty-returns.
aar_lightweight.follow_up: c158 = optional confirming lens Workflow on the post-c157 surfaces (independent rubric re-score). c159 = E4 decadal close (AAR + ADR-033 ratify + the graph prerender Perf fix). The homepage hero deploys with the E4 bundle.

notes: A measure/iterate cycle that closed the two still-undone design-spec themes (responsive/mobile coverage now guards the 40-node graph + vault detail at 320/375; the /network hero lead tightened) plus the c156 orphan-shelf 'didn't' (the 'Not yet linked' cluster now reads as a parked, subordinated holding area) and a join-scent lift (band-4 front-loads a commitment/safety cue row). The operator-added homepage thread returned the original aDNA DNA-helix-from-computers banner concept in the current Tokyo Night register: a cloned helix runner generated candidates, the operator picked the helix->network bridge (r1_H2_v1), and it swapped into the homepage hero — gate-10/11 green, Perf-neutral. Rubric GREEN with node_clarity + join_scent nudged up. 86/86 gates; axe 0 AA both modes; commit-only; image-gen $0.20.
